contextBlurb: Housing in the Northwest Territories is primarily administered by Housing NWT (formerly the NWT Housing Corporation), which manages approximately 2,900 homes across 32 communities. Key programs include Public Housing, Homeownership Programs, and CMHC-funded initiatives. Recent activity includes new modular duplex construction in remote communities and memoranda of agreement with First Nations on community housing planning.

legislation.title: Builders' Lien Act (replacing Mechanics Lien Act)
legislation.summary: The Builders' Lien Act and its supporting regulations came into force on September 1, replacing the former Mechanics Lien Act, affecting construction and housing-related liens in the NWT.

legislation.title: Public Housing Program – Housing NWT
legislation.summary: Housing NWT operates a public housing program providing rental units to eligible residents across 32 NWT communities, with tenant rights and responsibilities governed by a Tenant Handbook and subject to an appeal process.

legislation.title: GNWT Smoking Ban in Public Housing Units
legislation.summary: The GNWT implemented a smoking ban in all Housing NWT-owned and operated units, including public housing, as a health and safety policy for tenants.

legislation.title: Housing Appeal Committee – NWT Housing Corporation
legislation.summary: Provides a formal appeal process for applicants, tenants, and clients of the Homeownership Programs and Public Housing Program administered by Housing NWT.

legislation.title: Memorandum of Agreement on Housing with Acho Dene Koe First Nation
legislation.summary: Housing NWT and the GNWT signed a memorandum of agreement with Acho Dene Koe First Nation to establish a Housing Working Group, prioritizing community housing planning and coordinated program delivery.

legislation.title: CMHC Housing Programs and Funding in NWT
legislation.summary: A suite of federally co-funded housing programs delivered through Housing NWT covering construction, repair, and affordability initiatives across Northwest Territories communities.

legislation.title: New Partnership in Fort Good Hope – Community-Led Housing and Trades Training
legislation.summary: A partnership established to improve housing conditions in the Sahtu region by building local trades capacity and supporting community-led housing solutions in Fort Good Hope.

legislation.title: Smoking Ban in Public Housing Units
legislation.summary: The GNWT implemented a smoking ban in all Housing NWT-owned and operated units, including public housing units, to improve health and safety conditions for tenants.

legislation.title: Monthly Core Need Income Thresholds per Community 2021
legislation.summary: Establishes income thresholds by community used to determine eligibility for homeownership, rental support, and repair and maintenance housing programs administered by Housing NWT.
